script:
I used to rent a tiny house with a creepy unfinished basement.
The landlord told me not to worry about the light switch down there because “it never worked anyway.”
For months I avoided that basement unless I absolutely needed to grab something.
One night I went down to get an old laptop.
As soon as I reached the bottom step, the basement light suddenly turned on by itself.
Not flickered.
Turned on.
Bright and steady.
I froze because the switch was behind me, at the top of the stairs.
There was no one else in the house.
I ran back up, slammed the door, and sat on the couch trying to calm down.
Then the light turned off.
It stayed off the rest of the night.
The next morning I checked the switch.
It was still broken.
